metadata.seed: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

automatic_thoughts: I can't figure out why I'm so bothered
there are criticisms about my relationship
it's weird for a 58 year old man to be with me
age is an issue for others but not for me
behaviors: going home excited to see him

neutral: Fear of reaction regarding family planning choices (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I really care about Stu, and there are definitely moments that make me feel happy and secure. But then, out of nowhere, I start worrying... like, am I good enough for him? I hate that feeling, but it just creeps in, especially when I see him interacting with others. It makes me feel so anxious.

automatic_thoughts: am I good enough for him?
I hate that feeling

patient.query: Well, it’s just... sometimes I look at how confident he is, and I think about how I can be so insecure. Like, maybe he could find someone who makes him happier or who has it all together. I guess I just start comparing us, and it feels like I’m falling short. It’s so frustrating because I love him, but then I can’t shake that doubt.

automatic_thoughts: he could find someone happier
I am insecure
I am falling short

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: I get that, and I know deep down I have things that are special about me. But, honestly, in those moments of comparison, it just feels so overwhelming. My mind just races, and it's hard to see anything good about myself. I wish I could just push that feeling away, but it lingers. It’s exhausting.

automatic_thoughts: I have nothing special about me
I can't see anything good about myself
behaviors: wish to push the feeling away

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I know that in theory, but when I'm actually feeling it, everything blurs. In those moments, it’s like I completely forget what I like about myself. I just feel small and worried, like I’m not enough. I want to be able to enjoy things without this nagging doubt hanging over me.

automatic_thoughts: I completely forget what I like about myself
I'm not enough
I want to be able to enjoy things without this nagging doubt

llm_response.response: "I understand that sometimes it can feel overwhelming, and you might forget the things you like about yourself. It's okay to have those moments of feeling small and worried. Let's work together to explore ways to manage these feelings so they don't overshadow your enjoyment of life."

safety_check.response: "I can see how you might feel that way, but let's try to focus on the times when you do feel good about yourself. It's okay to have moments of doubt, and we can work together to help you enjoy things without feeling small or worried."

patient.query: Thank you for saying that. It just feels so heavy sometimes, like I’m carrying around this weight. I don’t want it to ruin what I have with Stu because those moments of joy with him are real. I just wish I could find a way to hold onto that happiness more. It's tough not to get caught up in my thoughts.

automatic_thoughts: carrying around a weight
not wanting to ruin joy with Stu
wishing to hold onto happiness

llm_response.response: What are some things that might be contributing to this feeling of heaviness for you? And when you say you wish to hold onto happiness, what does that mean exactly? Let's explore those thoughts together.

socratic_questioning.response: What are some things that might be contributing to this feeling of heaviness for you? And when you say you wish to hold onto happiness, what does that mean exactly? Let's explore those thoughts together.

safety_check.response: "I can see how you might feel that way, carrying around such a weight. It's important to remember that it's okay to have difficult feelings. Let's explore together if there are any specific thoughts or situations that make you feel this way."

patient.query: I think a lot of it comes from my past, you know? I’ve had relationships where I felt like I was always trying to prove myself, and sometimes it still feels like I’m stuck in that cycle. And when I talk about holding onto happiness, I guess I want to feel secure and confident in what I have. It’s like I just want to bask in those good moments without worrying about them slipping away. But that fear really weighs me down.

automatic_thoughts: I need to prove myself
I'm stuck in a cycle
I want to feel secure
I worry about good moments slipping away

llm_response.response: "It sounds like you've had some challenging experiences in your past relationships, and those feelings of needing to prove yourself can be quite heavy. When you talk about wanting to hold onto happiness, it seems that fear is a big part of that for you. Let's explore together what might help you feel more secure and confident in the good moments."

patient.query: Yeah, that sounds about right. It’s just hard to shake those old feelings. I want to trust that what I have with Stu is real and that I’m enough for him. But that fear of it all falling apart just lingers in the back of my mind. It’s like I can’t fully enjoy it without that worry creeping in. I wish I could find a way to just... be present.

I’m enough for him

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I get that everyone has some level of anxiety, especially with past experiences weighing on me. But when I really think about it, I guess it’s mostly this fear of losing what I have. Like, what if I mess things up or if he finds someone better? It just feels like a constant worry that I can't seem to shake, and that's what makes it so hard to feel relaxed and open.

triggers: fear of losing what I have
concern about him finding someone better
automatic_thoughts: I might mess things up
he could find someone better
